title: [lichen_l2.c:786-787] replay_table_init called without clearing active windows first
description:
Gap found: Replay table cleanup sequence not reviewed.

Why this was missed: Focus was on peer table, not replay table in disable path.

The actual bug: In lichen_l2_enable() disable path (line 786), lichen_replay_table_init() is called. According to replay.h semantics, this should reinitialize the table structure.

However, if replay windows contain dynamically allocated state (which they may depending on LICHEN replay implementation), calling init() without a cleanup function could leak memory.

Looking at the pattern: peer_table is explicitly zeroed with secure_zero (lines 791-795), but replay_table just calls init(). If lichen_replay_table_init() is a simple memset/zero operation, this is fine. If it assumes the table is already clean, there could be issues.

Need to verify: does lichen_replay_table_init() handle a dirty table, or does it assume fresh memory?

Priority 3 - potential resource leak depending on replay.h implementation.
close_reason: Fixed: Documented that lichen_replay_table_init() handles stale state via memset - no dynamic allocation in replay table. Added comments at all three call sites explaining this is safe on dirty tables.
